title: CSI SU / SD — Scroll Up / Scroll Down (pan)
summary: SU (final 'S') scrolls the contents of the scroll region up by Pn lines (new blank lines appear at the bottom); SD (final 'T') scrolls down by Pn lines (blank lines appear at the top). The cursor position is unchanged. Both default Pn to 1.

attribution.note: xterm ctlseqs: CSI Ps S = Scroll up Ps lines (SU); CSI Ps T = Scroll down Ps lines (SD). Default Ps = 1. (Note: CSI Ps ; Ps ; ... T with parameters is the xterm mouse highlight-tracking initiation, distinct from bare SD.)
ECMA-48 defines SU (final 'S') and SD (final 'T') as scrolling the page/region by Pn lines, default 1.

frame.note: CSI + single Pn + final byte: 'S' scroll-up (0x53), 'T' scroll-down (0x54). Pn defaults to 1; scoped to the scroll region.

terminator_detail.note: No string terminator; the CSI sequence ends at its final byte. Parameters Ps are decimal, ';' (0x3B) separated.

params.name: SU — Scroll Up (S)
params.meaning: CSI Pn S scrolls the scroll region up Pn lines; the top Pn lines scroll off and Pn blank lines appear at the bottom. The cursor stays put.

params.name: SD — Scroll Down (T)
params.meaning: CSI Pn T scrolls the scroll region down Pn lines; the bottom Pn lines scroll off and Pn blank lines appear at the top.

gotchas: BARE 'CSI Ps T' (one parameter) is SD; but 'CSI Ps ; Ps ; Ps ; Ps ; Ps T' (five parameters) is xterm's Initiate Highlight Mouse Tracking — parameter count disambiguates. Avoid emitting SD with stray extra parameters.
SU/SD pan the SCROLL REGION (DECSTBM); content outside the region is not moved.
New blank lines take the current SGR background color.
The cursor position is unchanged by SU/SD, unlike LF/RI which can move the cursor.

v1_smoke_test.asserts: SU ('S') and SD ('T') render as byte-exact single-parameter CSI sequences; the SD-vs-mouse-highlight ambiguity is noted.
